engineering.translation: For those whose ignorance has been destroyed (nāśitam) by self-knowledge, awareness — like the sun (āditya-vat) — illuminates the supreme. The chapter pivots. Verses 5.14-5.15 named the condition; 5.16 names the resolution. Knowledge dispels ignorance; the dispelling itself reveals the supreme. The engineering analog: the engineer who has done substrate-work — architectural reading, systems-thinking, real understanding of how the codebase, team, customer-loop, and infrastructure interrelate — no longer needs hero-narratives. The structural understanding illuminates. The sun-image is precise: not a torch, not a flashlight; the sun, indiscriminate and total in its illumination.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A staff engineer who has spent two years deeply reading the codebase — its history, its decisions, its accumulated patterns — joins an architecture review for a Postgres-vector-DB migration. The other engineers in the room are debating the trade-offs between three architectural options. She listens for ten minutes, then names the structural pattern that connects all three options to a common Datadog observability constraint nobody has surfaced. The room reorganises around her observation. She did not produce a hero-narrative; she made the structural pattern visible. The substrate-knowledge she had developed across two years was the sun-image of the verse: it illuminated the supreme — the structural reality the team needed to see — without her having to claim individual genius. The ajñāna of 5.15 had veiled the knowledge; the substrate-work was the destruction of the ajñāna; the illumination followed.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader hears 'knowledge illuminates' as 'reading more books makes you a senior engineer.' The verse's jñāna is structural realisation, not information-accumulation. The substrate-knowledge has to be the kind that produces structural seeing, not the kind that produces facts.
engineering.counter_example: When an engineer claims the sun-image without having done the substrate-work — when the structural seeing is asserted rather than earned — the verse does not apply. The verse's nāśitam is structurally significant; the destruction of ignorance is what produces the illumination.
engineering.implication: Audit your last three architecture reviews. Where did your contribution come from substrate-knowledge versus from positional claims? The verse names the difference.
engineering.quotable_line: Substrate-knowledge is the sun-image: indiscriminate, total illumination, without the engineer having to perform a hero-narrative.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 5.16 engineering layer drafted from triangulated source pack. Anchored to chapter-5 thesis: equivalence-of-paths-with-karma-yoga-preference, decoupled identity, sama-darśana, internal-bliss-source, brahma-nirvāṇa-as-operational-rung-with-honest-STRETCHED. Cross-references to chapters 2-4 preserved.
